name: Myanmar
avoid: June to September is the wettest stretch, with slower road transfers, flooded streets and rougher beach conditions
March and April are extremely hot across Yangon, Mandalay and Bagan, making daytime temple visits draining
Myanmar's political and security situation remains volatile after the 2021 coup, so check current government advisories, curfews and domestic flight suspensions before every leg
stopoverNote: Yangon is still the easiest international gateway, but domestic transport schedules can change with little notice; keep buffers between flights, buses and beach legs.

cities.name: Yangon
cities.notes: Colonial downtown, the golden Shwedagon skyline, teahouse culture and the country's most practical transport hub

cities.name: Bagan
cities.notes: Thousands of brick stupas, sunrise balloon skies, lacquerware workshops and easy e-bike temple circuits

cities.name: Mandalay
cities.notes: Royal palace history, monastery craft traditions, Amarapura sunsets and a good staging point for upper Myanmar

cities.name: Inle Lake
cities.notes: Longtail boat days, leg-rowing fishers, stilt villages, Shan food and cooler highland air around Nyaung Shwe

cities.name: Ngapali Beach
cities.notes: Palm-lined Bay of Bengal sands, seafood shacks, bikeable fishing villages and Myanmar's classic slow beach finish

itinerary.sim: MPT or ATOM are the easiest prepaid SIMs for this route; expect roughly ₹350–₹900 for enough data for maps, hotel messaging and flight re-checks, but keep screenshots because mobile networks can be disrupted.

Yangon.name: Yangon
Yangon.note: Two days is enough for the pagodas and downtown core; add a third only if you want museum time, slower tea-shop hopping or a cushion before an onward domestic flight.

days.theme: Golden pagodas, lakeside Yangon and a first taste of teahouse culture

activities.name: Shwedagon Pagoda foreigner ticket
activities.tip: This is the real 20,000 MMK foreigner fee, so carry cash and dress conservatively for shoulder and knee coverage.

activities.name: People's Park and western Shwedagon viewpoints
Karaweik Palace exterior and lakeside sunset
Yangon teahouse breakfast and evening milk tea rounds

days.theme: Colonial downtown, markets and pagoda-lined city history

activities.name: Yangon Circular Train loop segment
activities.tip: Ride only a short central stretch if time is tight; a full loop is long and best done in daylight.

Bagan.name: Bagan
Bagan.note: Two full days covers the major temple plains well; add a third if you want a balloon flight, Mount Popa detour or slower sunrise and sunset repeats.

days.theme: Temple-zone classics by e-bike across Old Bagan and Nyaung U

activities.name: Bagan Archaeological Zone ticket
activities.tip: This is the real 30,000 MMK fee introduced after the 2023 increase and is the main mandatory heritage charge for foreign visitors.

activities.name: E-bike rental for the temple plain
Shwesandaw or Pyathada sunset viewpoint circuit
activities.tip: Climbing rules change, so follow local signage and use designated mounds or terraces instead of closed pagoda tops.

meals: Weather Spoon's Bagan Restaurant and Bar

days.theme: Sunrise balloons, lacquerware and Irrawaddy-side temple life

activities.name: Optional hot air balloon flight over Bagan
Nyaung U Market browse and snack stop

Mandalay.name: Mandalay
Mandalay.note: Two days works best if you want both the palace-monastery core and Amarapura; add a third for Sagaing, Mingun or a slower craft-focused day.

days.theme: Royal Mandalay, monastery carvings and hilltop panoramas

activities.name: Mandalay Zone ticket including Mandalay Palace
activities.tip: This reflects the common 15,000 MMK foreigner fee used for the palace and nearby heritage sites.

activities.name: Mandalay Palace moat and palace core
Mandalay Hill sunset ascent
activities.cost: Included or donation-based depending on checkpoint

days.theme: Sacred images, bridge sunsets and easy Amarapura side trips

activities.name: Taungthaman Lake wooden boat at sunset
Jade Market or Zegyo Market browse

Inle Lake.name: Inle Lake
Inle Lake.note: Keep two days for a full lake boat circuit and one slower shore-based Shan day; a third day helps only if you want trekking or a long transfer buffer.

days.theme: Longtail boat day through pagodas, gardens and stilt villages

activities.name: Inle Lake foreigner zone fee
activities.tip: This is the standard 25,000 MMK charge collected around Nyaung Shwe for foreign visitors entering the lake zone.

activities.name: Private longtail boat hire for the main lake circuit
activities.tip: The real daily boat rate usually lands around 25,000–35,000 MMK depending on route length and whether you add Indein.

activities.name: Phaung Daw Oo Pagoda
activities.cost: Included in zone fee or donation-based

days.theme: Indein pagodas, Nyaung Shwe lanes and slower Shan highland time

activities.name: Indein Pagoda complex by boat detour
Shan cooking class or tomato salad workshop
Bicycle rental around Nyaung Shwe vineyards and farms

Ngapali Beach.name: Ngapali Beach
Ngapali Beach.note: Two nights is the right finish for most Myanmar trips; Ngapali is more about rest than ticking off sights, and onward connections can change quickly in bad weather.

days.theme: Beach reset, cycling and easy seafood by the bay

activities.name: Ngapali Beach swim and sunset walk
Bicycle rental to nearby fishing villages
Traditional massage near the beach road
Beach lounger or resort day-use spend

days.theme: Boat excursion morning and a quiet final Bay of Bengal sunset

activities.name: Pearl Island or nearby snorkeling boat trip
Ngapali market or village produce stop
Coconut and palm-lined beach walk to Gyeiktaw
Kayak or paddle hire through a resort desk

connections.method: JJ Express overnight bus or Myanmar National Airlines flight to Nyaung U
Irrawaddy river boat, shared van or private car
Overnight bus to Nyaung Shwe or flight to Heho
Flight via Yangon on Myanmar National Airlines or MAI

extras: Best timing is November to February for cooler, drier weather; temple dawns in Bagan and beach afternoons in Ngapali are both easier then.
Myanmar's political situation remains unstable, so confirm every bus, flight and hotel directly and keep a backup route in case checkpoints, curfews or cancellations affect the next leg.
Carry crisp cash because foreign cards fail often, ATMs can go offline and some pagoda tickets still prefer exact MMK notes.
Domestic flights save time but are not always punctual; build same-day slack rather than tight airport-to-bus or flight-to-beach connections.
Respect dress codes at Shwedagon, Sule, Mahamuni and lake monasteries: covered shoulders, covered knees and shoes off before entering sacred areas.
If your government currently advises against non-essential travel to Myanmar, reconsider the trip or keep to the main tourist corridor only after reviewing the latest notice.
